expeditionTargets.label: Local Scavenge Sweep
expeditionTargets.playerHint: Send one free crew member to sweep the near edge of the safe route for useful material.

expeditionTargets.label: Survivor Cave Watch
expeditionTargets.playerHint: Post a small team by the cave route to trade news, read movement, and bring back leads.

expeditionTargets.label: Far Ruins Probe
expeditionTargets.playerHint: Push a larger team past the close bubble lanes for stronger material returns and dangerous clues.

resonanceRecipes.label: Bassline Overtone
resonanceRecipes.playerHint: Fold field echoes back into the Crystal so Bassline workers push reach harder.

resonanceRecipes.label: Chorus Carrier
resonanceRecipes.playerHint: Use recovered carrier noise to make Chorus power and life support less starved.

resonanceRecipes.label: Harmonic Lattice
resonanceRecipes.playerHint: Turn the strangest residues into Harmonics stability for advanced base scaling.

resonanceRecipes.label: Field Attunement
resonanceRecipes.playerHint: Tune staging and field response so future expeditions return faster and cleaner.

storyBeats.label: Road to Base
storyBeats.body: The last safe road is a scar through dead ground. Somewhere ahead, a weak musical hum is still holding back the static.

choices.label: Follow the low signal
choices.response: You stay on the broken road because the distant hum feels like the only promise left.

choices.label: Keep moving through the ash
choices.response: You refuse to stop moving. If there is shelter ahead, momentum will find it first.

storyBeats.label: First Glimpse
storyBeats.body: From the ridge, you finally see it: a broken complex wrapped in a thin blue halo. The Base is still standing for now.

choices.label: Study the lights
choices.response: The light pulses in time with the hum. Someone built this place to keep something worse outside.

choices.label: Scan the ruins
choices.response: The outer shell is wrecked, but the center still breathes. The Base might be dying, not dead.

storyBeats.label: Enter the Bubble
storyBeats.body: Crossing the boundary changes everything. The pressure drops, the noise thins, and the Crystal's pulse becomes a direction instead of a warning.

choices.label: Trust the sound wall
choices.response: The pressure eases as soon as you cross the edge. The Bubble is weak, but it is real.

choices.label: Test the air first
choices.response: Your hand trembles at the border. Inside the Bubble, the noise of the world finally steps back.

storyBeats.label: Investigate Base
storyBeats.body: The first sweep is about triage. Find what still works, what is beyond repair, and what the Base needs before it collapses completely.

choices.label: Search for surviving systems
choices.response: If anything still runs here, it will tell you what can be saved.

choices.label: Trace the hum through the walls
choices.response: The sound has a source. If you can reach it, the Base might answer back.

storyBeats.label: Explore Base
storyBeats.body: Now go deeper. The first repair loop is buried somewhere inside the ruin, along with the pieces you need to wake the Base back up.

choices.label: Look for tools and salvage
choices.response: You push deeper, looking for anything that can turn ruins into repairs.

choices.label: Look for livable rooms
choices.response: If more people are coming, they will need more than a miracle. They will need a place to stay.

storyBeats.label: Restore Studio
storyBeats.body: The Studio is the first room worth saving. If you can bring it back, the Base can start singing again.

storyBeats.label: Build Fire Pit
storyBeats.body: The Fire Pit is less about heat than rhythm. People gather around steady signals before they trust walls and wiring.

storyBeats.label: Reach Survivor Cave
storyBeats.body: If the Bubble holds long enough, its edge will brush the cave where the next survivors are hiding.

storyBeats.label: First Survivor Recruited
storyBeats.body: The first recruit is proof the Base is more than a shelter. It is becoming a place people can choose.

storyBeats.label: Await Survivor Arrival
storyBeats.body: Signal the route. Keep the Base stable. A promise only matters if someone can safely walk into it.

storyBeats.label: Stabilize the Base
storyBeats.body: The Base is alive, but not safe yet. Power, morale, housing, and sound all need to hold at once now.

models.label: Bubble coverage is budgeted from stored Bassline.
Harmonics generation rate determines the active tier.
Continuous Harmonics output boosts efficiency before tiers.
Collection pauses at cap instead of losing overflow.
Recruit cost follows the Good Vibes curve anchors.
Manual power requests participate in LIFO brownouts.
The station improves tolerance to brownouts.
Research can offset some life-support cost.
Research reduces the Harmonics tier thresholds.
Plains use the baseline impedance profile.

blockers.label: Overflow is lost when Bassline storage is full.

flows.label: Crystal Bassline staffing generates stored Bassline.
Removing Moss adds passive Bassline.
Crystal upgrades drain Bassline while builders work.

modelRefs.label: Bubble coverage is budgeted from stored Bassline.

notes: Bassline is both a stored resource and the active bubble budget anchor.

presentation.playerHint: Stored Bassline holds the bubble open and feeds Crystal upgrades.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Spending too far into Bassline budget will put bubble coverage at risk.

unlocks.label: Restore Studio to unlock Chorus production.

blockers.label: Chorus staffing is locked until the Studio is restored.
Overflow is lost when Chorus storage is full.

flows.label: Crystal Chorus staffing generates stored Chorus.
Life support always consumes Chorus when active staff exceed the free allowance.
Manual stations consume Chorus while powered.

notes: Chorus is the power rail for stations and life support.

presentation.playerHint: Chorus is the power rail for stations and life support.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: If Chorus falls behind upkeep, brownouts will start unpowering requested stations.

unlocks.label: Build Resonance Chamber to unlock Harmonics production.

blockers.label: Harmonics staffing is locked until the Resonance Chamber is built.
Overflow is lost when Harmonics storage is full.

flows.label: Crystal Harmonics staffing generates stored Harmonics.
Harmonics feeds tier thresholds and output multipliers.

modelRefs.label: Harmonics generation rate determines the active tier.
Continuous Harmonics output boosts efficiency before tiers.

notes: Harmonics is a structural efficiency resource, not a direct upkeep pool.

presentation.playerHint: Harmonics boosts Crystal efficiency, field strength, and power tolerance.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Ignoring Harmonics leaves the base efficient only on paper and fragile under pressure.

blockers.label: Stone collection pauses at cap.

accessRules.label: Base scavenging is available on the Base tile.

flows.label: Scavenge Crew gathers Stone from Base stock while online.
Base projects and processing spend Stone upfront.

modelRefs.label: Collection pauses at cap instead of losing overflow.

notes: Stone is an untuned material and the first hard construction gate.

presentation.playerHint: Stone is the first hard gate for restoring the base and building stations.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: If Stone stock runs dry, construction and processing stall immediately.

unlocks.label: Explore Base unlocks Water collection.

blockers.label: Water collection pauses at cap.

accessRules.label: Water collection is a Base-tile action.

flows.label: Water Crew gathers from Base Water stock while online.
Base Water stock regenerates passively.
Processing and polishing consume Water upfront.

modelRefs.label: Collection pauses at cap instead of losing overflow.

notes: Water is an untuned material with a separate Base stock and player pool.

presentation.playerHint: Water supports polishing and advanced processing once the base starts opening up.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Low Water slows advanced improvements even when the rest of the economy looks healthy.

unlocks.label: Build Fire Pit to start the Vibes loop.

flows.label: Fire Pit produces Vibes passively and from staffed crew.
Recruitment spends Vibes upfront.
Overcrowding creates Bad Vibes pressure against the pool.

modelRefs.label: Recruit cost follows the Good Vibes curve anchors.

notes: Vibes can go negative and directly control early recruitment pacing.

presentation.playerHint: Vibes are the social fuel for early recruitment.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Bad Vibes from overcrowding will push recruitment farther away.

blockers.label: Crystal roles compete for shared free slots.

accessRules.label: Crystal staffing is only available at the Base.

flows.label: Assigned staff convert time into Bassline output.

notes: The Hero can assign here without consuming a crew slot.

presentation.playerHint: Assign Bassline staff when you need safer reach or Crystal upgrades.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Too little Bassline output leaves the bubble vulnerable to spending.

unlocks.label: Restore Studio to unlock Chorus staffing.

blockers.label: Studio must be restored before Chorus staffing exists.

accessRules.label: Crystal staffing is only available at the Base.

flows.label: Assigned staff convert time into Chorus output.

presentation.playerHint: Assign Chorus staff when power pressure or brownouts begin to appear.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Without Chorus staffing, power demand will outgrow supply quickly.

unlocks.label: Build Resonance Chamber to unlock Harmonics staffing.

presentation.playerHint: Assign Harmonics staff when the base needs better efficiency, tiers, and tolerance.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: No Harmonics means weaker output multipliers and less brownout protection.

blockers.label: Construction crew are locked while building or restoring.

blockers.label: Fire Pit must be built before Vibes staffing exists.

flows.label: Scavenge Crew gathers Stone while online.

blockers.label: Water collection is locked until the tutorial Explore completes.

presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Delaying the Fire Pit delays recruitment and the first real growth jump.

presentation.playerHint: Objectives translate the current story and system state into a playable next move.
Shows where the first-session arc currently stands.
The highest-priority thing the player should complete next.
Shows the next meaningful progression step after the active goal.
Surfaces the main current gate in the first playable loop.
Shows the next system or story layer the player will gain.
A condensed recommendation derived from the current progression state.
Highlights the most important current risk in the first session.
Construction turns staffing and resources into permanent base progress.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: No builders means all progress stalls immediately.

presentation.playerHint: The Crystal is only surfaced once the overgrowth is cleared enough to uncover it.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Before the Crystal is uncovered, its full control surface stays hidden.

presentation.playerHint: The Hero can idle, staff a band, or leave to perform world actions.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: World actions temporarily pull the Hero away from band support.

presentation.playerHint: The map shows current bubble reach and nearby progression landmarks.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: If Bassline falls too low, the bubble can contract and lose reach.

presentation.playerHint: Crystal slots are shared across Bassline, Chorus, and Harmonics staffing.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: No free slots means the Crystal cannot scale without upgrades.

presentation.playerHint: Crystal output level increases all three band tracks.
Field Polish improves how efficiently Bassline becomes bubble coverage.
Shows how much active bubble coverage the current Bassline pool is sustaining.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Spending too deeply into Bassline can force the bubble to contract.

presentation.playerHint: Shows the primary current source for Bassline.
Shows the primary current demand on stored Bassline.
Summarizes the current bubble risk created by Bassline pressure.
Current Chorus generation per second.
Powered stations consume Chorus while active.
Shows the primary current source for Chorus.
Shows the primary current power-side Chorus demand.
Summarizes current brownout risk driven by Chorus pressure.
Harmonics tier reflects how far efficiency and resilience have progressed.
Harmonics efficiency boosts the rest of the base economy.
Shows the primary current source for Harmonics.
Shows where Harmonics-side progress is currently being invested.
Summarizes the current risk of leaving Harmonics understaffed.
Chorus powers the base. Harmonics improves throughput and resilience.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Brownouts will shut down the last requested powered station first.

presentation.playerHint: Shows what the Hero is currently assigned to do.
Shows whether the Hero is idle, active, or tied up on a world action.
Builders determine how quickly active construction can progress.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: No builders means every construction project stalls.

presentation.playerHint: Shows the main current input feeding construction progress.
Shows the active current construction demand.
Shows the main thing currently stopping construction from moving.
Shows the main current input to the power layer.
Shows the main current Chorus demand in the power network.
Shows the main current blocker in the power and processing layer.
Shows the current Chorus upkeep from active powered stations.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: High upkeep relative to Chorus income increases brownout pressure.

presentation.playerHint: Shows how many staffed roles are currently contributing to life support demand.
Active staff increase Chorus upkeep through life support.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: If Chorus runs thin, life support contributes to brownout pressure.

presentation.playerHint: Brownouts appear when Chorus cannot sustain all requested power and life support.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Increase Chorus or reduce powered demand to stabilize the base.

presentation.playerHint: The Base panel gathers supplies, housing, recruitment, and social pressure.
Shows how much scavengable stone remains around the base.
Shows how much collectable water remains in the local source.
Shows the main current source feeding the Base layer.
Shows the main current demand on Base-side materials and Vibes.
Shows the main current blocker in the Base layer.
Vibes pay for new survivors once the cave route is open.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: If Vibes stall, crew growth stalls with them.

presentation.playerHint: Run controls handle save/load and offline return visibility for the current play session.
The Studio provides bunks. Housing limits how fast the crew can grow safely.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: If bunks run short, Bad Vibes will start building up.

presentation.playerHint: Housing shows whether current bunks can support everyone at the base.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Missing bunks will degrade morale and eventually crew performance.

presentation.playerHint: Bad Vibes track the pressure created by overcrowding and unstable housing.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: If morale drain rises, crew efficiency will fall.

presentation.playerHint: Crew speed reflects how much Bad Vibes are slowing the base down.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Low crew speed slows construction, gathering, and the whole first-session arc.

presentation.playerHint: Scavenge Crew convert the surrounding ruins into Stone for repairs and projects.
Fire Pit Crew turn your built fire into Vibes for recruitment.
Water Crew collect and refill the water loop for repairs and upgrades.
Recruiting survivors spends Vibes and expands your crew capacity.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: If Vibes are low, recruitment stalls.

presentation.playerHint: The Studio is the first major repair and unlocks housing plus Chorus.
The Fire Pit determines whether the Vibes loop is actually online.
This shows pending recruits and remaining instant recruit stock.
Housing status warns when the crew outgrows the available bunks.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Overcrowding creates Bad Vibes over time.

presentation.playerHint: The cave route opens once the Bassline bubble reaches it safely.
App eyebrow label.
Player-facing view.
Debug and inspection view.
Catalog and graph inspection view.
Current run protagonist.
Bassline-focused Hero class track.

Harmonics-focused Hero class track.
Shows current Viral Load and strain tier.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: If Viral Load crosses the point of no return, forced retreat starts immediately.

presentation.playerHint: Tracks the Hero wound pool separately from Viral Load.
Shows how much safe margin remains before forced retreat or how long retreat will take.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: A shrinking window means the Hero is almost out of safe return budget.

presentation.playerHint: Recovery starts only once the Hero is back in the Studio and slows during severe brownouts.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Brownouts can slow or stop recovery at the Studio.

presentation.playerHint: Forced returns leave Echo Scars behind even when the Hero survives.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Echo Scars stack and will matter more as the world layer opens up.

presentation.playerHint: Viral Load thresholds reduce Hero work and movement before full forced return.
presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Higher strain makes the Hero slower and less effective outside safety.

presentation.primaryRiskCopy: Spend less Bassline or grow supply to avoid shrink.
